title: ZK-STARKS, StarkNet,  & CAIRO
content: ## ZK-STARKS An alternative to ZK-SNARKS that does not rely on a trusted ceremony but has the following drawbacks: - larger proof sizes - longer to verify Users would accept this drawback because it means that they do not have to rely on a trusting that a ZK-SNARK network has correctly and safely (this means discarding the secrets used in the trusted ceremony). ## What is StarkNet? From the docs: > A decentralized, permissionless Layer 2 (L2) validity rollup that scales Ethereum by utilizing STARK-based zero-knowledge proofs to bundle thousands of transactions off-chain for verified settlement on the mainnet. ### Architecture [Source](https://www.certik.com/blog/an-introduction-to-the-cairo-programming-language) **Key Observations** - Accounts are SMART Contracts not just a pair of keys - **The sequencer is run by StarkWave!** It's not truly decentralized. - [Decentralization is being worked towards though](https://www.starknet.io/blog/starknet-grinta-the-architecture-of-a-more-decentralized-future/) **The fact that accounts are natively built into the platform is a big differentiator from ETH** > Doesn't ETH already have this? Not really. ETH has *Externally Owned Accounts (EOAs)* which are controlled by a pair of keys. Everything from the account addr. to the authority to execute txns comes from said keys. ## Reading List - [A good overview on StarkNet and Cairo](https://www.certik.com/blog/an-introduction-to-the-cairo-programming-language) - [StarkNet's S-two](https://docs.starknet.io/learn/S-two-book/introduction)
